text.title: Journey Mapping Procedure
text.summary: Step-by-step procedure to apply the journey-mapping methodology end-to-end.

step.name: define-scope
step.action: Name one persona, one journey, explicit start and end points.
step.decision-gate: If two personas in scope, split into two maps before continuing.

step.name: gather-research
step.action: Collect multi-source evidence: interview transcripts, support tickets, analytics funnels, surveys, customer-support staff input.
step.output: Citable corpus indexed by evidence id.
step.decision-gate: If no research evidence exists for any stage, mark "no data" and surface it as a research gap; do NOT invent.

step.name: identify-stages
step.action: Break the journey into 4-8 stages named from the user's perspective; reject company-pipeline names.

step.name: populate-action-rows
step.action: For each stage fill actions, touchpoints, thoughts. Each cell carries an evidence id or literal "no data".

step.name: map-emotions
step.action: Score each stage 1-5 with a label and evidence id; preserve sharp dips, prompt for variance.
step.decision-gate: If arc is monotone smooth, re-prompt explicitly for variance before accepting.

step.name: identify-pain-and-opportunities
step.action: For each stage list pain points with citations; for each pain point write at least one opportunity linked to a backlog id.
step.decision-gate: If any opportunity lacks a `linked_backlog_id`, block until linked.

step.name: validate-and-flag-review
step.action: Run scripts/validate-journey-mapping.py; surface "ready for owner review" flag.
